info.title: Ecomail API
info.description: Programmatically manage your email marketing — lists, subscribers, campaigns, automations, and more.

tags.name: Automations
tags.description: Trigger automation pipelines and read automation statistics.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Create, send, and analyze email campaigns.

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: Manage sending domains and their verification status.

tags.name: Feeds
tags.description: Refresh product feed data used for personalization.

tags.name: Lists
tags.description: Manage subscriber lists and the subscribers within them.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Search for contacts across your account.

tags.name: Subscribers
tags.description: Read subscriber profiles, events, and activity logs.

tags.name: Tracker events
tags.description: Send custom tracking events for contacts.

tags.name: Transactional emails
tags.description: Send transactional messages and read their statistics.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Configure webhook URLs for real-time event delivery.

get.description: Retrieve all automations in your account.
Retrieve all actions (nodes) within a given automation.
Retrieve detail of a specific action (node) within a given automation.

post.description: Run a specific automation for the given email/subscriber.

get.description: Retrieve aggregate statistics for a specific automation.
Return statistics for a specific automation with each subscriber and their individual events.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: Filter by event type. One of: `open`, `send`, `unsub`, `soft_bounce`, `click`, `hard_bounce`, `out_of_band`, `spam`.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Filter from date onwards in `YYYY-MM-DD` format (UTC).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default is `100`, maximum is `5000`.

get.description: Returns all campaigns with pagination and filtering.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Column to sort results by. Allowed values: `id`, `sent_at`, `changed_at`, `title`, `status`.

recepient_lists.description: Array of list IDs or an object with segments. See the example above for the segments format.

get.description: Returns detailed campaign information including statistics for sent campaigns.

recepient_lists.description: Array of list IDs or an object with segments.

post.description: Puts the campaign in the sending queue immediately.
Sends a test version of a draft campaign to up to 5 recipients.

emails.description: Recipients of the test email. At least 1 and at most 5 email addresses.

merge.description: Optional map of merge-tag values used to personalize the test email. Keys are merge-tag names, values are their replacements; empty values are ignored.

errors: You can not do this because you have a blocked account
Only draft campaigns can be tested.
You have exceeded daily limit of sent test emails of 50.

get.description: Returns aggregated statistics for a specific campaign.
Returns statistics for a specific campaign with each subscriber and their individual events.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Filter from date onwards (`YYYY-MM-DD` in UTC).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Results per page. Maximum and default is `100`.

get.description: Retrieves performance statistics for a specific segment across all campaigns it was sent to.

parameters.name: segmentId
parameters.description: Segment ID. Can be retrieved from the [Show list detail](/api-reference/lists/show) endpoint.

get.description: Search and browse the campaign log — sends, opens, clicks, bounces, unsubscribes, complaints, and other events. Filter by subscriber email, event types, and date range.

parameters.name: events[]
parameters.description: Filter by event types. Can be specified multiple times. Possible values: `send`, `open`, `click`, `bounce`, `hard_bounce`, `soft_bounce`, `out_of_band`, `unsub`, `spam`, `spam_complaint`, `not_opened`, `not_clicked`, `open_no_click`, `sms_sent`, `sms_inv_nr`, `sms_accepted`, `whatsapp_sent`, `whatsapp_failed`.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter events from this date (inclusive, ISO format e.g. `2025-01-01`).

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter events up to this date (inclusive, ISO format e.g. `2025-12-31`). Must be after `date_from` if provided.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default `20`, maximum `100`.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Column to sort by. Possible values: `occured_at`, `id`, `event`. Default `occured_at`.

post.description: Import discount coupons into a default or specific folder.

folderId.description: Folder ID. If not provided, coupons are imported into the default folder.

update_existing.description: Update existing coupons if they already exist.

delete.description: Delete discount coupons from a default or specific folder.

folderId.description: Folder ID. If not provided, coupons are deleted from the default folder.

codes.description: Array of coupon code strings to delete.

get.description: Retrieve all domains in your account.

post.description: Add a new domain to your account.

delete.description: Remove a domain from your account.

get.description: Trigger a refresh of a product feed.
Trigger a refresh of a data feed.

example.errors: Feed can be refreshed just once per hour

get.description: Retrieve all subscriber lists in your account.

post.description: Create a new subscriber list in your account.

from_name.description: Sender name used in emails sent from this list.

from_email.description: Sender email address used in emails sent from this list.

sub_success_page.description: URL to redirect to after submitting a sign-up form.

sub_confirmed_page.description: URL to redirect to after confirming a sign-up email.

unsub_page.description: URL to redirect to after unsubscribing from the list.

double_optin.description: Whether sign-up confirmation (double opt-in) is required.

conf_subject.description: Subject line of the confirmation email.

conf_message.description: HTML content of the confirmation email.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific list, including custom fields, groups, segments, and subscriber counts.

from_name.description: Sender name used in emails sent from this list.

from_email.description: Sender email address used in emails sent from this list.

sub_success_page.description: URL to redirect to after submitting a sign-up form.

sub_confirmed_page.description: URL to redirect to after confirming a sign-up email.

unsub_page.description: URL to redirect to after unsubscribing from the list.

double_optin.description: Whether sign-up confirmation (double opt-in) is required.

conf_subject.description: Subject line of the confirmation email.

conf_message.description: HTML content of the confirmation email.

get.description: Retrieve all segments created in a specific list.
Retrieve a paginated list of subscribers from a specific list.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of subscribers per page, up to 5000. We recommend keeping this at a lower number (e.g. 4000) depending on the amount of data each subscriber has.

get.description: Retrieve a specific subscriber from a list by their email address.

errors: Subscriber with email foo@bar.cz was not found in the list

get.description: Retrieve a specific subscriber from a list by their phone number.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone number of the subscriber. Must be a valid international phone number.

post.description: Subscribe a new contact to a specific list.

status.description: Subscriber status. `1` = subscribed, `2` = unsubscribed, `4` = hard bounce, `5` = spam complaint, `6` = unconfirmed.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values. Limited to 5000 characters total. Reserved system merge tags are ignored. If a field doesn't exist, a new field is created. Supported types: `string`, `date`, `json`, `int`, `float`, `url`. Float precision is limited to 3 decimal places. Keys are case-insensitive — they are trimmed and lowercased on input, so `SHOP`, `shop` and ` Shop ` are treated as the same field. Two keys that differ only in case or whitespace in the same request are rejected as duplicates.

tags.description: Subscriber tags. Limited to 2000 characters total and 50 characters per tag. Content overwrites current tags. Only updates if the parameter is present and `update_existing` is `true`. To clear all tags, send an empty array.

groups.description: Group assignments. Overwrites current values. To keep existing selections, send the current values together with new ones.

trigger_autoresponders.description: Trigger automations when the subscriber is added.

trigger_notification.description: Send a notification about the new subscriber if configured in list settings.

update_existing.description: Update the subscriber if they already exist in the list.

skip_confirmation.description: Skip double opt-in confirmation. If `false`, the system sends a double opt-in email and the subscriber is added with status `6` (unconfirmed).

resubscribe.description: Force resubscribe if the subscriber was previously unsubscribed (status `2`). If `status` is provided in `subscriber_data`, it takes precedence. For resubscribe to work, the status must be `1`.

subscriber_data.custom_fields: Custom fields must be an array with unique keys (case-insensitive)
Subscriber custom fields must be an array with max 5000 bytes

post.description: Subscribe multiple contacts to a specific list in a single request.

subscriber_data.description: Array of subscriber objects. Limited to 3000 subscribers per call — subscribers above the limit are ignored.

status.description: Subscriber status. `1` = subscribed, `2` = unsubscribed, `4` = hard bounce.

update_existing.description: Update subscribers that already exist in the list.

resubscribe.description: Force resubscribe if the subscriber was previously unsubscribed.

trigger_autoresponders.description: Trigger automations when subscribers are added.

custom_fields: Custom fields must be an array with unique keys (case-insensitive)
Subscriber custom fields must be an array with max 5000 bytes

delete.description: Unsubscribe a subscriber from a specific list.

email.description: Email address of the subscriber to unsubscribe.

put.description: Update an existing subscriber in a specific list.

email.description: Email address of the subscriber to update.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values. Limited to 5000 characters total. Keys are case-insensitive — they are trimmed and lowercased on input, so `SHOP`, `shop` and ` Shop ` are treated as the same field. Two keys that differ only in case or whitespace in the same request are rejected as duplicates.

tags.description: Subscriber tags. Limited to 2000 characters total and 50 characters per tag. Content overwrites current tags.

subscriber_data.custom_fields: Custom fields must be an array with unique keys (case-insensitive)
Subscriber custom fields must be an array with max 5000 bytes

put.description: Update multiple existing subscribers in a specific list in a single request.

subscriber_data.description: Array of subscriber objects to update. Limited to 3000 subscribers per call.

email.description: Email address of the subscriber to update.

status.description: Subscriber status. `1` = subscribed, `2` = unsubscribed, `4` = hard bounce.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values. Limited to 5000 characters total. Keys are case-insensitive — they are trimmed and lowercased on input, so `SHOP`, `shop` and ` Shop ` are treated as the same field. Two keys that differ only in case or whitespace within the same subscriber are rejected as duplicates.

tags.description: Subscriber tags. Limited to 2000 characters total and 50 characters per tag. Content overwrites current tags.

allow_resubscribe.description: Allow resubscribing previously unsubscribed contacts when `status` is set to `1`.

subscriber_data.0.custom_fields: Custom fields must be an array with unique keys (case-insensitive)
Subscriber custom fields must be an array with max 5000 bytes

get.description: Retrieve all Spark recommenders configured for your account.

post.description: Create a new Spark recommender for a specific shop and product feed. Training starts automatically after creation.

feed_id.description: The product feed ID to use for recommendations.

errors: Recommender for this shop and feed already exists.

delete.description: Remove a Spark recommender by its ID. Returns the updated list of remaining recommenders.

parameters.name: recommender_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recommender to delete.

post.description: Search for a subscriber globally across the account by email address.

query.description: Must be a valid email address.

get.description: Get subscriber details globally across all lists in the account.
Retrieve a paginated list of transactions (purchases) for a given subscriber email address, sorted by timestamp descending. Each transaction includes its items.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default `100`, maximum `500`.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of tracker events for a given subscriber email address, sorted by ID descending.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default `20`, maximum `100`.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of campaign log entries for a given subscriber email address, sorted by occurrence date descending.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default `20`, maximum `100`.

parameters.name: events[]
parameters.description: Filter by event types. Can be specified multiple times. Possible values: `send`, `open`, `click`, `hard_bounce`, `soft_bounce`, `out_of_band`, `unsub`, `spam`, `spam_complaint`.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of automation (pipeline) log entries for a given subscriber email address, sorted by timestamp descending by default.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Default `20`, maximum `100`.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Column to sort by. Possible values: `timestamp`, `id`, `pipeline_id`. Default `timestamp`.

delete.description: Permanently delete a subscriber from the database.

get.description: Retrieve all email templates in your account.
Retrieve a specific template by its ID.

put.description: Update an existing email template by its ID.

inline_css.description: Whether to inline CSS styles into the HTML.

post.description: Track a custom event for a subscriber. Supplements the JavaScript tracker.

email.description: Subscriber's email address. The subscriber must already exist in your contact list.

label.description: Event label — a human-readable name or identifier (e.g. a product name, page title).

property.description: Event property — a custom key or identifier (e.g. a product SKU, category slug).

value.description: Event value. Can be a plain string, a JSON-encoded string, or a JSON object/array (objects and arrays will be JSON-encoded automatically on our side). Maximum length is **20,000 characters** (after JSON encoding, if applicable).

get.description: Retrieve a single tracker event by its ID.

post.description: Send a transactional email with custom content.

message.description: The message object containing all email details.

from_email.description: Sender's email address. Must be from a verified domain.

text.description: Plain text content. Either `text` or `html` is required.

html.description: HTML content. Either `text` or `html` is required.

global_merge_vars.description: Array of merge tag objects for personalization.

post.description: Send a transactional email using a pre-defined template.

message.description: The message object containing all email details.

template_id.description: ID of the template to use.

from_email.description: Sender's email address. Must be from a verified domain.

global_merge_vars.description: Array of merge tag objects for personalization.

get.description: Get statistics for transactional emails for the past 6 months.
Get statistics for double opt-in emails for the past 6 months.

post.description: Create a new transaction for a subscriber.

shop.description: Name of your shop, used in conditionals in the app. Case sensitive. An empty value results in not being able to select the shop in segments.

amount.description: Total amount including tax and shipping.

categories.description: List of product categories. Limited to 100 characters per category and 20 categories per item. Currently not visible in the Ecomail app — use the `category` parameter for now.

price.description: Total price for the given quantity.

tags.description: Optional item tags. Limited to 5000 characters per account.

transaction.order_id: The transaction.order id has already been taken.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of transactions with optional filters.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of results per page. Maximum `500`.

post.description: Create multiple transactions in a single request.

transaction_data.description: Array of transaction objects, each containing a `transaction` and `transaction_items` object with the same structure as the [single create endpoint](/api-reference/transactions/create).

transaction.description: Transaction details. See [Create a transaction](/api-reference/transactions/create) for all available properties.

transaction_items.description: Array of transaction items. See [Create a transaction](/api-reference/transactions/create) for all available properties.

put.description: Overwrite an existing transaction with new data.

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Order ID of the transaction to update.

transaction.description: Transaction details. Same structure as the [Create a transaction](/api-reference/transactions/create) endpoint.

amount.description: Total amount including tax and shipping.

transaction_items.description: Array of transaction items. Same structure as the [Create a transaction](/api-reference/transactions/create) endpoint.

price.description: Total price for the given quantity.

delete.description: Delete a single transaction by order ID.

transaction_data.description: Optional filter to match the transaction by shop.

shop.description: If provided, the transaction is only deleted if it matches this shop value.

delete.description: Delete multiple transactions in a single request.

transaction_data.description: Array of transaction identifiers to delete.

order_id.description: Order ID of the transaction to delete.

shop.description: If provided, the transaction is only deleted if it matches this shop value.

transaction_data: The transaction data may not have more than 1000 items.

post.description: Set the target webhook URL for campaign statistics.

get.description: Get the currently configured webhook URL.

delete.description: Remove the currently configured webhook URL.
